name: dqsegdb2

metadata.name: dqsegdb2
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 or later (GPLv3+)

documentation:
# DQSEGDB2

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/dqsegdb2.svg)](http://badge.fury.io/py/dqsegdb2)
[![Conda version](https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/dqsegdb2.svg)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/dqsegdb2/)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/dqsegdb2.svg)](https://choosealicense.com/licenses/gpl-3.0/)
[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/136390328.svg)](https://zenodo.org/badge/latestdoi/136390328)
![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/dqsegdb2.svg)

[![Build status](https://git.ligo.org/duncanmmacleod/dqsegdb2/badges/main/pipeline.svg)](https://git.ligo.org/duncanmmacleod/dqsegdb2/-/pipelines)
![Code coverage](https://git.ligo.org/duncanmmacleod/dqsegdb2/badges/main/coverage.svg)
[![Documentation status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/dqsegdb2/badge/?version=latest)](https://dqsegdb2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

DQSEGDB2 is a simplified Python implementation of the DQSEGDB API as defined in
[LIGO-T1300625](https://dcc.ligo.org/LIGO-T1300625).

This package only provides a query interface for `GET` requests, any users
wishing to make `POST` requests should refer to the official `dqsegdb` Python
client available from <https://github.com/ligovirgo/dqsegdb/>.

## Installation

For best results, please install from [conda-forge](https://conda-forge.org/):

```shell
conda install -c conda-forge dqsegdb2
```

On Unix systems, you can also install directly from PyPI:

```shell
pip install dqsegdb2
```

## Basic Usage

```python
from dqsegdb2.query import query_segments
print(query_segments('G1:GEO-SCIENCE:1', 1000000000, 1000001000))
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.query._url
documentation: Construct the full URL for a query to `host` using an API path func.

functions.name: dqsegdb2.query.query_ifos
documentation:
Query for all defined interferometers (IFOs).

# Parameters

host

: The URL of the DQSegDB server; if `None`
  {func}`~dqsegdb2.utils.get_default_host` will be used to discover
  the default host.

raw

: Return the full JSON response from the request.

request_kwargs

: Other keyword arguments are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`.

# Returns

`set`

: If `raw=False`: the set of all known IFO prefices.

`dict`

: If `raw=True`: the full JSON response is returned.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.query import query_ifos
>>> query_ifos()
{'H1', 'V1', 'G1', 'K1', 'L1'}
>>> query_ifos(raw=True)
{"query_information": {
     "start": 0,
     "server_timestamp": 1234567890,
     "end": 0,
     "server_elapsed_query_time": "0.12345",
     "include": [],
     "uri": "/dq",
     "api_version": "1.2.34",
     "server": "segments"},
 "Ifos": ["G1", "H1", "K1", "L1", "V1"]}
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.query.query_names
documentation:
Query for all defined flags for the given `ifo`

# Parameters

ifo

: The interferometer prefix for which to query.

host

: The URL of the DQSegDB server; if `None`
  {func}`~dqsegdb2.utils.get_default_host` will be used to discover
  the default host.

raw

: Return the full JSON response from the request.

request_kwargs

: Other keyword arguments are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`.

# Returns

`set`

: If `raw=False` the set of all define flag names in the format
  `{ifo}:{name}`.

`dict`

: If `raw=True`: the full JSON response is returned.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.query import query_names
>>> query_names('X1')
{'G1:GEO-FLAG_1', 'G1:GEO-FLAG_2'}
>>> query_names('X1', raw=True)
{'results': ['GEO-FLAG_1', 'GEO-FLAG_2'], 'query_information': ...}
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.query.query_versions
documentation:
Query for defined versions for the given flag.

# Parameters

flag

: The name for which to query.

host

: The URL of the DQSegDB server; if `None`
  {func}`~dqsegdb2.utils.get_default_host` will be used to discover
  the default host.

raw

: Return the full JSON response from the request.

request_kwargs

: Other keyword arguments are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`.

# Returns

`list` of `int`

: If `raw=False` (default), the list of defined versions
  for the given flag.

`dict`

: If `raw=True`: the full JSON response is returned.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.query import query_versions
>>> query_versions('G1:GEO-SCIENCE')
[1, 2, 3]
>>> query_versions('G1:GEO-SCIENCE', raw=True)
{'resource_type': 'version', 'version': [1, 2, 3],
 'query_information': ...}
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.query.query_segments
documentation:
Query for segments for the given flag in a `[start, stop)` interval.

# Parameters

flag

: The name for which to query, see \_Notes\_ for information on how
  versionless-flags are queried.

start

: The GPS start time.

end

: The GPS end time.

host

: The URL of the DQSegDB server; if `None`
  {func}`~dqsegdb2.utils.get_default_host` will be used to discover
  the default host.

coalesce

: If `True`, coalesce the segmentlists returned by the server,
  and restrict them to lie fully within the `[start, end)`
  request segment, otherwise return the 'raw' result,
  default: `True`.
  This option is ignored if `raw=True` is given.

raw

: Return the full JSON response from the request.
  If an explicit version is not given, the result will be a
  `list` of JSON responses, one for each discovered version.

request_kwargs

: Other keyword arguments are passed to {func}`igwn_auth_utils.get`.

# Returns

`dict`

: If `raw=False` (default): a `dict` with the following keys

  - `'ifo'` - the interferometer prefix (`str`)
  - `'name'` - the flag name (`str`)
  - `'version'` - the flag version (`int`)
  - `'known'` - the known segments (`~ligo.segments.segmentlist`)
  - `'active'` - the active segments (`~ligo.segments.segmentlist`)
  - `'metadata'` - a `dict` of flag information (`dict`)
  - `'query_information'` - a `dict` of query information (`dict`)

`object`

: If `raw=True` is given, **and** the flag name includes an explicit
  version, the result will be the raw JSON response from the single
  request, otherwise a `list` of JSON responses will be returned.

# Notes

If `flag` is given without a version (e.g. `'X1:FLAG-NAME'`) or the
version is given as `'*'` (e.g. `'X1:FLAG-NAME:*'`) the result of
the query will be the intersection of queries over all versions found
in the database.
In that case the `'metadata'` and `'query_information'` in the output
will be preserved for the highest version number only (if `raw=False`).

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.query import query_segments
>>> query_segments('G1:GEO-SCIENCE:1', 1000000000, 1000001000)
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.requests.get_json
documentation:
Perform a GET request and return JSON.

# Parameters

args, kwargs

: all keyword arguments are passed directly to
  {meth}`igwn_auth_utils.requests.get`

# Returns

data

: the URL reponse parsed with {func}`json.loads`

# See also

igwn_auth_utils.requests.get

: for information on how the request is performed

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api._query_string
documentation: Encode a query string, ignoring params with value `None`.

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api._query_path
documentation: Format a path and query parameters for a DQSegDB query.

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api._query_params
documentation: Decorate an API path function to attach a query string.

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.ifos_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for available IFOs.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import ifos_path
>>> ifos_path()
'/dq'
```
-- /dq API paths

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.flags_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for flags for a given IFO.

# Parameters

ifo

: The prefix (two-character) for an interferometer.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import flags_path
>>> flags_path('G1')
'/dq/G1'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.versions_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a specific flag.

# Parameters

ifo

: The prefix (two-character) for an interferometer.

flag

: The name (excluding the IFO prefix) of a flag.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import versions_path
>>> versions_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE')
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.resources_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a specific flag version.

# Parameters

ifo

: The prefix (two-character) for an interferometer.

flag

: The name (excluding the IFO prefix) of a flag.

version

: The version number of the flag.

s

: The GPS start time of the interval of interest.

e

: The GPS end time of the interval of interest.

include

: The resources to include in the returned data.
  Typically one or more of `'active'`, `'known'`, or
  `'metadata'`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import resources_path
>>> resources_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1'
>>> resources_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1, s=1, e=2)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1?s=1&e=2'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.metadata_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for metadata for a specific flag.

# Parameters

ifo

: The prefix (two-character) for an interferometer.

flag

: The name (excluding the IFO prefix) of a flag.

version

: The version number of the flag.

s

: The GPS start time of the interval of interest.

e

: The GPS end time of the interval of interest.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import resources_path
>>> metadata_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/metadata'
>>> metadata_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1, s=1, e=2)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/metadata?s=1&e=2'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.active_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for active segments for a specific flag.

# Parameters

ifo

: The prefix (two-character) for an interferometer.

flag

: The name (excluding the IFO prefix) of a flag.

version

: The version number of the flag.

s

: The GPS start time of the interval of interest.

e

: The GPS end time of the interval of interest.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import resources_path
>>> active_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/active'
>>> active_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1, s=1, e=2)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/active?s=1&e=2'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.known_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for known segments for a specific flag.

# Parameters

ifo

: The prefix (two-character) for an interferometer.

flag

: The name (excluding the IFO prefix) of a flag.

version

: The version number of the flag.

s

: The GPS start time of the interval of interest.

e

: The GPS end time of the interval of interest.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import resources_path
>>> known_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/known'
>>> known_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1, s=1, e=2)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/known?s=1&e=2'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.insert_history_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for the insert history for a specific flag.

# Parameters

ifo

: The prefix (two-character) for an interferometer.

flag

: The name (excluding the IFO prefix) of a flag.

version

: The version number of the flag.

s

: The GPS start time of the interval of interest.

e

: The GPS end time of the interval of interest.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import resources_path
>>> insert_history_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/insert_history'
>>> insert_history_path('G1', 'GEO-SCIENCE', 1, s=1, e=2)
'/dq/G1/GEO-SCIENCE/1/insert_history?s=1&e=2'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.report_path
documentation:
Return the API path to quey for a data-quality report.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import report_path
>>> report_path()
'/report'
```
-- /report API paths

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.report_flags_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a report of all flags.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import report_flags_path
>>> report_flags_path()
'/report/flags'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.report_coverage_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a coverage report of all flags.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import report_coverage_path
>>> report_coverage_path()
'/report/coverage'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.report_db_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a database statistics report.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import report_db_path
>>> report_db_path()
'/report/db'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.report_process_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a report of processing information.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import report_process_path
>>> report_process_path()
'/report/process'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.report_known_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a report of all flags
in the known state.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import report_known_path
>>> report_known_path()
'/report/known'
>>> report_known_path(s=1, e=2)
'/report/known?s=1&e=2'
```

functions.name: dqsegdb2.api.report_active_path
documentation:
Return the API path to query for a report of all
flags in the active state.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from dqsegdb2.api import report_active_path
>>> report_active_path()
'/report/active'
>>> report_active_path(s=1, e=2)
'/report/active?s=1&e=2'
```
